explanation: This structured NL property captures the requirement that in PCV or PSV mode, after each inspiration and only if inspiratory pause is not activated, a lung recruitment procedure (RM) can be initiated if the GUI indicates it by activating the RMButton. The choice 'whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause, _ABSTRACT_VAR2_' models the trigger context of the RM: after an inspiration ends, only if not in inspiratory pause, and in either PCV or PSV mode. The 'upon RMButton, eventually satisfy RM' enforces that, upon the GUI requesting RM, a Recruitment Maneuver must eventually be initiated.

decision2_substring: if it is required by the GUI
decision3_substring: initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM)
output_structured_natural_language: whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause, upon RMButton, eventually satisfy RM

explanation: The requirement specifies that in PCV and PSV modes, at the end of inspiration, and only if inspiratory pause is not required, it must be possible to initiate a lung recruitment maneuver (RM) if commanded by the GUI. To capture this, we check that whenever (i) either PCVMode or PSVMode is active, (ii) the end of inspiration occurs, and (iii) inspiratory pause is not required, then if RMButton is pressed, the RM procedure (RM) should become active. This structured NL property captures the system being in correct modes, in the correct phase, and the dependencies on GUI commands and lack of pause requirement.

explanation: The property specifies that, in both PCV and PSV ventilation modes (as identified by atomic propositions PCVMode and PSVMode), at the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and if there is no inspiratory pause (i.e., !inspiratoryPause), a lung recruitment maneuver (RM) can be initiated if it is requested by the GUI (RMButton). This translation combines all these conditions appropriately. The chosen template is 'whenever bool_exp2, _ABSTRACT_VAR2_' for the condition being met and 'eventually satisfy bool_exp3' to permit the action (initiate RM). It does not strictly require RM to always be initiated, but it must be possible (the system allows or enables it under those circumstances).

explanation: The requirement states that, in PCV and PSV modes, at the end of inspiration, provided that an inspiratory pause is not being requested, it should be possible to initiate the lung recruitment procedure (Recruitment Maneuver, RM) if requested by the GUI. The corresponding atomic propositions are: 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode' for being in either of the two modes, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' for the end of inspiration, '!inspiratoryPauseButton' for inspiratory pause not being required, and 'RMButton' to represent the GUI requesting RM. According to the property template, we want to express that whenever the system is in either PCV or PSV mode, upon reaching the end of the inspiration phase, and provided an inspiratory pause is not required, if the GUI requests it (RMButton), then it should be possible to initiate the RM recruitment maneuver. The structured NL reflects this by making the RM conditionally eventually true under the gating conditions.

explanation: The requirement states that in PCV or PSV mode, and specifically at the end of an inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if there is no inspiratory pause (not inspiratoryPause), it must be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) when requested by the GUI (RMButton). This requires the condition that either PCVMode or PSVMode is active, and at the end of inspiration, without an inspiratory pause, if the RMButton is pressed, the RM can be initiated. The structured NL reflects this conjunction of mode, phase, pause, and GUI input.

explanation: The requirement specifies that in either PCV or PSV mode, at the end of inspiration, and provided inspiratory pause is not required, it should be possible to initiate the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if requested by the GUI. The relevant atomic propositions are: PCVMode and PSVMode for the modes, inspiratoryPhaseEnd for the end of inspiration, inspiratoryPause for the inspiratory pause requirement, and RMButton for the GUI request. Combining these, the trigger condition is (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton. The property specifies that whenever this trigger occurs, it must be possible to activate RM, i.e., RM can be set true. The structured NL captures this as a trigger-action property: whenever the mode is PCV or PSV and inspiration ends and there is no inspiratory pause required and the GUI requests a Recruitment Maneuver, then it is possible to initiate RM (Recruitment Maneuver).

explanation: This structured NL captures the requirement that a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) may be initiated if, at the end of an inspiration in either PCV or PSV mode, an inspiratory pause has not been requested (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is not active), and the GUI requests the RM (RMButton is active). The requirement is conditioned both on mode (PCVMode or PSVMode) and end of inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), with an additional constraint that inspiratory pause is not set. Only under all those conditions is it possible (or allowed) to initiate RM, as dictated by the RMButton. The expression ensures exactly when RM can be started, aligning the structured NL with the source intent: 'at the end of an inspiration and if inspiratory pause is not required, it shall be possible to initiate (activate) RM, if required by GUI.'

explanation: The requirement says that in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) and PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) modes, at the end of an inspiration and if an inspiratory pause is not required by the GUI, the user should be able to initiate a 'Recruitment Maneuver' (RM) if also requested by the GUI. In terms of atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode' represent the two required ventilation modes, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' indicates the end of inspiration, 'inspiratoryPause' and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' together cover situations where inspiratory pause is not required/set, and 'RMButton' indicates the GUI is requesting an RM. The structured NL captures all these preconditions as a conjunction, and specifies that when these hold, it must be possible to initiate (i.e., activate) the RM (represented by 'RM'). This translation follows the logic and intent of the NL requirement: whenever all mode and gating conditions are satisfied, the RM must be possible.
decision1_substring: In PCV and PSV mode, at the end of an inspiration and if inspiratory pause is not required, it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI.
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI.
output_structured_natural_language: whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton, eventually satisfy RM
